operator_edits_applied: Wrapped evidence_quote values in typographic quotation marks
Trimmed speculative analyst_notes to first sentence only
Promoted jurisdiction to top-level filing_jurisdiction field

patterns_extracted.rule: Always include a top-level 'case_reference' field combining case_title, case_number, and jurisdiction in the format: '<title> | No. <number> | <jurisdiction>'
patterns_extracted.example_before: No top-level reference field; case_title, case_number, and jurisdiction appear only as separate fields
patterns_extracted.example_after: "case_reference": "Marcus A. Hendricks v. Nexigen | No. 24-CV-08821 | Superior Court CA"

patterns_extracted.rule: Normalise all date values in relevant_dates to ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD); replace slash-separated or free-text dates with the ISO equivalent
Wrap all evidence_quote field values in escaped double-quote characters so the quoted text is visually distinct from surrounding JSON string content
Limit analyst_notes to a single declarative sentence; remove qualifying clauses and hedges beyond the first period to keep the note actionable
patterns_extracted.example_before: "analyst_notes": "This summary is a first-pass analysis ... and should be verified against the original complaint and unattached exhibits before any reliance for litigation purposes."
patterns_extracted.example_after: "analyst_notes": "This summary is a first-pass analysis based solely on five retrieved evidence passages."

patterns_extracted.rule: Always include a top-level 'filing_jurisdiction' field that duplicates the jurisdiction value for quick visual scanning without requiring lookup inside nested fields
patterns_extracted.example_before: jurisdiction appears only inside the draft body as "jurisdiction": "Superior Court of California..."
patterns_extracted.example_after: "filing_jurisdiction": "Superior Court of the State of California, County of Los Angeles, Central District"

active_patterns.rule: Always include a top-level 'case_reference' field combining case_title, case_number, and jurisdiction in the format: '<title> | No. <number> | <jurisdiction>'
